steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what point the author makes about Spanish. Trace both examples in the passage: "coyote" traveled Nahuatl → Spanish → English, and "llama" traveled Quechua → Spanish → English. In both cases Spanish sits in the middle, passing an Indigenous word along to English. The correct answer must capture that intermediary role.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It has borrowed words from Indigenous languages and contributed words to them.: The passage shows Spanish borrowing from Indigenous languages, but nothing in the passage says Spanish contributed words back to those languages. The second half of this claim is not supported. ✗
  • Choice B — It adopted Nahuatl and Quechua words in approximately equal numbers.: The passage gives exactly one example from each language. Two examples cannot support a claim about proportions or equality across the whole vocabulary. This overgeneralizes. ✗
  • Choice C — It has served as a medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English.: Both examples follow the same path: Indigenous language → Spanish → English. Spanish is the medium — the passage explicitly says each word "entered English through Spanish." This matches the passage exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — Its contribution to English vocabulary roughly equals the collective contribution by Indigenous languages.: The passage never compares the size of Spanish’s and Indigenous languages’ contributions to English. No proportional claim is made anywhere. ✗
That is the correct answer.
